# UC-13.7.2 · Fluentd Output Plugin Error Rate by Destination

## Description

Splunk, S3, and Kafka outputs fail independently; aggregate error counters per `plugin_id` show which leg of the pipeline is dropping bytes even when the agent process stays healthy.

## Value

Shortens isolation time for multi-destination logging and prevents mis-blaming the wrong team.

## Implementation

Normalize `plugin_type` (`out_forward`, `out_splunk`, `out_s3`). If only snapshots exist, use `delta` between polls via `streamstats`. Enrich `match_pattern` with team ownership lookup.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:plugins"
| where match(plugin_type, "^out")
| eval errs=coalesce(num_errors, error_count, retry_count)
| eval emitted=coalesce(emit_records, emit_count, 1)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(errs) as err_sum, sum(emitted) as out_sum by _time, plugin_id, match_pattern
| eval err_rate=round(100*err_sum/nullif(out_sum,0),4)
| where err_rate > 0.5 AND out_sum > 10000
| sort -err_rate
```

## Visualization

Stacked area (errors by output), line chart (err_rate), table of top plugin_id.

## References

- [Fluentd — Output plugins](https://docs.fluentd.org/output)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
